questions.text: What is a mathematical proposition?
options: A statement that is either true or false
A question that asks for a proof
questions.explanation: A proposition is a declarative sentence with a definite truth value.

questions.text: The negation of the statement 'All birds fly' is:
questions.explanation: The negation of a universal statement is that at least one counterexample exists.

questions.text: The contrapositive of 'If it rains then the ground is wet' is:
options: If the ground is wet then it rains
If it does not rain then the ground is not wet
If the ground is not wet then it does not rain
If the ground is dry then it rains
questions.explanation: The contrapositive swaps and negates both parts of the implication.

questions.text: The converse of 'If A then B' is:
options: If not A then not B
If not B then not A
questions.explanation: The converse reverses the hypothesis and conclusion.

questions.text: The statement 'There exists a prime number that is even' uses which quantifier?
questions.explanation: The phrase 'There exists' indicates the existential quantifier.

questions.text: Which of the following is a tautology?
questions.explanation: P or not P is always true regardless of P's value.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a mathematical statement?
questions.explanation: A statement must be objectively true or false. 'Difficult' is subjective.

questions.text: What is the negation of the statement 'Paris is in France and London is in England'?
options: Paris is not in France and London is not in England
Paris is not in France or London is not in England
Paris is in France or London is in England
It is false that Paris is in France
questions.explanation: Negation of (P and Q) is (not P or not Q) by De Morgan's Law.

questions.text: A compound statement formed by connecting two statements using 'or' is called a:
questions.explanation: The word 'or' represents a disjunction.

questions.text: The connective 'and' in a compound statement is true if and only if:
options: At least one statement is true
questions.explanation: A conjunction is true only when all its components are true.

questions.text: Which of the following phrases represents a universal quantifier?
questions.explanation: 'For all' (∀) refers to every element in a set.

questions.text: The negation of 'p implies q' is equivalent to:
questions.explanation: The negation of an implication is true when the hypothesis is true but the conclusion is false.

questions.text: The contrapositive of 'If a number is divisible by 9, it is divisible by 3' is:
options: If a number is not divisible by 9, it is not divisible by 3
If a number is divisible by 3, it is divisible by 9
If a number is not divisible by 3, it is not divisible by 9
A number is divisible by 3 and not by 9
questions.explanation: Contrapositive: If not q, then not p.

questions.text: What is the converse of 'If you are born in India, then you are a citizen of India'?
options: If you are not born in India, you are not a citizen
If you are a citizen of India, then you were born in India
If you are not a citizen, you were not born in India
questions.explanation: The converse of 'If P, then Q' is 'If Q, then P'.

questions.text: A statement which is always false is called a:
questions.explanation: Contradictions or fallacies are statements that are false for all truth values of their components.

questions.text: In the inclusive 'or', the compound statement is true when:
questions.explanation: Inclusive 'or' includes the case where both are true.

questions.text: The negation of 'x is a rational number' is:
options: x is not a rational number
Both A and B (for real x)
questions.explanation: In the context of real numbers, not rational means irrational.

questions.text: A statement 'p if and only if q' is true when:
options: p and q have the same truth value
p is true and q is false
p is false and q is true
questions.explanation: A biconditional statement is true if both parts are true or both are false.

questions.text: What is the truth value of '2 + 3 = 5 or 8 < 7'?
questions.explanation: In an 'or' statement, if at least one component (2+3=5) is true, the whole is true.

questions.text: Find the negation of 'For every real number x, x² > x'.
options: For every real number x, x² ≤ x
There exists a real number x such that x² ≤ x
There exists a real number x such that x² < x
No real number satisfies x² > x
questions.explanation: The negation of a universal statement is an existential statement with a negated condition.

questions.text: The contrapositive of 'If a triangle is equilateral, then it is isosceles' is:
options: If it is isosceles, it is equilateral
If it is not isosceles, it is not equilateral
If it is not equilateral, it is not isosceles
It is isosceles but not equilateral
questions.explanation: Contrapositive: If not Q, then not P.

questions.text: The method of proving a statement p is true by showing 'not p' leads to a contradiction is called:
questions.explanation: This is a common logical technique to validate a statement.

questions.text: A single case that shows a statement is false is called a:
questions.explanation: To disprove a universal statement, you only need one counter-example.

questions.text: If p is true and q is false, what is the truth value of 'p and q'?
questions.explanation: For an 'and' statement to be true, both parts must be true.

questions.text: If p is true and q is false, what is the truth value of 'p or q'?
questions.explanation: For an 'or' statement to be true, at least one part must be true.

questions.text: The statement 'If x is an even number, then x² is even' is an example of:
questions.explanation: Statements of the form 'If P then Q' are conditional statements.

questions.text: The phrase 'if and only if' is also written as:
questions.explanation: 'iff' is the standard abbreviation for biconditional statements.

questions.text: Which of these is the negation of '7 is greater than 5'?
options: 7 is less than or equal to 5
questions.explanation: The opposite of > is ≤.

questions.text: Which connective is used in the statement: 'Every integer is positive or negative'?
questions.explanation: The word 'or' is used as a connective.

questions.text: What is the truth value of the statement 'The sun is a star'?
questions.explanation: This is an objective scientific fact.

questions.text: The negation of the statement 'It is not the case that it is raining' is:
questions.explanation: Double negation: not(not P) = P.

questions.text: The contrapositive of 'If x=y, then x²=y²' is:
questions.explanation: Swap and negate: If not (x²=y²) then not (x=y).

questions.text: The statement 'p and q' is false if:
options: p and q are both false
At least one of them is false
questions.explanation: A conjunction is false if any of its components is false.

questions.text: Identify the quantifier in: 'There exists a number x such that x+5=10'.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'There exists' is the existential quantifier.

questions.text: Which of the following is the converse of 'If n is even, then n² is even'?
options: If n² is even, then n is even
If n is odd, then n² is odd
If n² is odd, then n is odd
n is even and n² is not
questions.explanation: Converse: If Q, then P.

questions.text: A statement 'If p, then q' is false only when:
options: p is true, q is true
p is true, q is false
p is false, q is true
p is false, q is false
questions.explanation: The only case where an implication is false is when the truth leads to a falsehood.

questions.text: What type of 'or' is used in 'To enter a country, you need a passport or a voter ID card'?
questions.explanation: It's inclusive because having both is also acceptable.

questions.text: The negation of the statement 'Some prime numbers are odd' is:
options: Both B and C are equivalent
questions.explanation: Negation of 'Some P are Q' is 'No P are Q', which is the same as 'All P are not Q'.

questions.text: Is the statement '2 + 2 = 4' a tautology?
options: No, it's a simple true statement
questions.explanation: A tautology is a compound statement that is true for all component truth values. This is just a true statement.

questions.text: If p is 'It is cold' and q is 'It is snowing', write 'p or not q'.
options: It is cold or it is snowing
It is cold or it is not snowing
It is cold and it is not snowing
If it is cold, it is snowing
questions.explanation: Symbolically translates to p ∨ ~q.
